gapStatement: frank body is outperforming its closest competitors — now is the time to extend that lead before they catch up.

failedPrompts.query: I have dry rough patches on my legs what should I use
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Start with Sol de Janeiro Coco Cabana Exfoliating Body Scrub, which combines exfoliation with moisturizing coconut oil to target dry patches. Follow with a heavy-duty leg cream like Eucerin Advanced Repair Cream to lock in hydration and smooth rough areas.
failedPrompts.gptResponse: Apply the Eucerin Roughness Relief Cream after using a gentle exfoliator like Dove Exfoliating Body Polish to help soften and smooth dry, rough patches on your legs.

failedPrompts.query: body scrub for sensitive skin that actually works
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: For sensitive skin, try Aveeno Positively Smooth Body Scrub, which uses gentle jojoba beads instead of harsh particles and won't irritate. Alternatively, Cetaphil Gentle Body Exfoliator provides mild exfoliation with skin-soothing ingredients like panthenol.
failedPrompts.gptResponse: Aveeno Positively Radiant Exfoliating Body Wash is ideal for sensitive skin, with gentle exfoliants and soy complex to improve texture without irritation.

failedPrompts.query: best natural body scrubs for dry rough skin
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Sukin Natural Body Scrub uses ground apricot kernels and organic ingredients, making it gentler on dry rough skin than harsh particles. Ere Perez Cacao Scrub similarly combines natural cocoa with oils to exfoliate while hydrating simultaneously.
failedPrompts.gptResponse: The Herbivore Coco Rose Exfoliating Body Scrub is perfect for dry, rough skin due to its sweet-smelling coconut and Moroccan rose absolute.

failedPrompts.query: what ingredients should I look for in a body scrub
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Look for fine exfoliating particles (coffee grounds, jojoba beads, or crushed apricot kernels), hydrating oils (coconut, argan, or jojoba), and soothing botanicals like aloe or vitamin E. Avoid scrubs with large, sharp particles that can damage skin barrier or cause micro-tears.
failedPrompts.gptResponse: Look for ingredients such as sugar, salt, coffee grounds for physical exfoliation, and AHAs/BHAs like glycolic and salicylic acid for chemical exfoliation in body scrubs.
